channel.title: BinderBrief Daily
channel.description: Daily commercial insurance and underwriting automation insights.

item.title: Insurance AI hiring rises 32% despite workforce cuts
item.description:
Insurance companies increased their hiring of artificial intelligence (AI) specialists by 32% last year despite a 2.2% overall reduction in staffing, according to research by Evident. This growth in AI roles supports automation, analytics, claims, underwriting, and fraud detection through integrated agentic systems connecting workflows like first notice of loss, triage, and policy checks. The shift to enterprise-wide AI deployment improves underwriting efficiency and operational productivity while reducing manual bottlenecks, impacting how insurers allocate workforce resources and manage risk.
Name: Peter Sonner, Company: BeInsure, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the insurance AI hiring article at BeInsure

item.title: Cyber insurance holds steady as AI sharpens attack sophistication
item.description:
The cyber insurance market, according to Brown & Brown Risk Solutions, is stable with modest softening and competitive capacity expansion over the last three years. The underwriting process now incorporates external threat intelligence and active assessment of implemented controls while brokers increasingly conduct pre-underwriting evaluations including risk framework mapping and client education. This evolution improves carrier underwriting accuracy, maintains broker relevance through deeper advisory roles, and sustains commercial margins amid growing AI-driven cyber threats and complex identity risks.
Name: Christopher Keegan, Company: Brown & Brown Risk Solutions, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Managing Director and Cyber and Technology National Practice Leader at Brown & Brown Risk Solutions

item.title: Digital insurance platform market to reach $432.18 bn by 2035
item.description:
The digital insurance platform market was valued at $156 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach $432 billion by 2035, growing at a 10.7% CAGR. This growth is driven by the integration of artificial intelligence, cloud computing, big data analytics, and automation across underwriting, claims processing, onboarding, and policy management systems. The adoption of these digital platforms improves underwriting accuracy, reduces claims costs, accelerates customer onboarding, and enhances scalable distribution, thereby increasing carrier underwriting efficiency and competitive capacity.
Name: Peter Sonner, Company: BeInsure, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article and insurance technology analyst.

item.title: NTT DATA Report Highlights Growing Insurance Risks and AI Adoption Challenges
item.description:
NTT DATA released its Insurtech Global Outlook 2026 report, highlighting growing uninsured losses including $171 billion in cyber losses projected to rise to $700 billion by 2030, and a 57% increase in liability claims. The report analyzes rising risks alongside AI adoption, noting that while 66% of insurance workers use AI tools, only 22% of insurers have fully implemented AI due to trust and governance challenges. This gap affects underwriting efficiency and operational costs, with AI offering potential cost savings of up to 35% and emphasizing the need for resilience, responsible AI, prevention-focused services, and partner ecosystems to manage rising risks and capacity limits.
Name: N/A, Company: NTT DATA, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Global IT services provider that produced the Insurtech Global Outlook 2026 report

item.title: Exclusive: AI liability MGA Ollive targets summer 2026 launch
item.description:
Ollive, an insurtech startup, plans to launch AI liability coverage for vendors in summer 2026 and is close to completing a $5 million seed funding round. The product targets AI-related vendor risks, though specific technical mechanisms or data sources are not disclosed in the article. This coverage aims to create a new capacity option for brokers and carriers managing AI liability exposures.
Name: Mia Macgregor, Company: Unknown, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article Name: Ricky Lamey, Company: Thomson Reuters, Email: ricky.lamey@thomsonreuters.com, Type: Individual, Info: Business development executive at Thomson Reuters Name: Andy Stone, Company: Thomson Reuters, Email: andy.stone@thomsonreuters.com, Type: Individual, Info: Sales manager at Thomson Reuters

item.title: Why proving AI works is no longer enough
item.description:
Allianz Partners’ chief transformation officer Clara Silvestri said insurers face challenges moving from AI pilots to full operational change despite widespread technology access. She explained that transformation requires redesigning processes and operating models around AI, integrating automation decisions with human intervention, demonstrated in Allianz Partners’ roadside assistance which uses AI voice agents to improve customer satisfaction. This operational embedding improves underwriting efficiency and customer experience but demands organisational change, affecting commercial broker margins and carrier capacity by shifting roles and reducing pilot-related inertia.
Name: Clara Silvestri, Company: Allianz Partners, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Transformation Officer at Allianz Partners overseeing AI and operational transformation in insurance processes

item.title: Insurtech Sixfold launches AI Underwriter after $30 mn funding
item.description:
Sixfold launched AI Underwriter following a $30 million Series B funding round led by Brewer Lane with strategic investments from Guidewire, Bessemer Venture Partners, and Salesforce Ventures. The platform uses autonomous AI agents that learn from carrier-specific underwriting standards, broker interactions, portfolio context, and prior decisions to support end-to-end underwriting workflows with real-time recommendations. This technology reduces underwriting processing times by up to 97%, improves hit ratios by at least 15%, and increases gross written premium per underwriter by up to 30%, enhancing carrier underwriting efficiency and capacity.
Name: Alex Schmelkin, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Sixfold responsible for the AI Underwriter platform development Name: Peter Sonner, Company: BeInsure, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article and analyst covering insurtech developments

item.title: UK Consumers Worried About AI Fraud Threat -
item.description:
CRIF released research showing that 75% of UK consumers are now worried about AI-powered fraud, up from 24% in 2025, and 40% believe banks and insurers are not adequately protecting them. The new CRIF anti-fraud solution, launching in Q3 2026, will use AI to automate checks in KYC, KYB, and AML processes by detecting patterns and inconsistencies missed by manual or legacy systems. This technology aims to improve carrier underwriting efficiency and reduce fraud-related losses, addressing concerns that financial providers lag behind rising AI-enabled threats.
Name: Sara Costantini, Company: CRIF, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Regional Director for the UK and Ireland at CRIF responsible for AI fraud research and solutions.

item.title: Akur8 and Verisk partner to modernise insurance rating
item.description:
Akur8, a global actuarial AI platform, has joined the Verisk Strategic Alliance partner network to support customer-imported ISO Electronic Rating Content (ISO ERC) within its Deploy rating engine. This integration licenses Verisk’s ISO ERC, providing insurers electronic access to advisory loss costs, rules, and forms data directly inside Akur8's rating system for streamlined workflows. The partnership aims to improve insurer go-to-market speed by reducing manual implementation errors, enhancing underwriting governance, and modernizing rating operations.
Name: Samuel Falmagne, Company: Akur8, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Akur8, a global actuarial AI platform Name: Troy Smith, Company: Verisk, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice President, Ratings Solutions at Verisk, involved in ISO ERC and rating solution delivery

item.title: AI integrations gain ground as VisitorsCoverage launches travel insurance protocol server
item.description:
VisitorsCoverage has launched the Treppy MCP server, a technology platform that enables AI-powered travel booking and concierge tools to access travel insurance quotes and coverage from multiple providers through a single integration. The platform uses Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol framework to provide a standardized interface for real-time insurance data retrieval, policy comparison, and eligibility checks across carriers. This technology reduces integration complexity and costs for travel platforms, potentially increasing commercial broker margins and improving carrier underwriting efficiency by streamlining digital insurance distribution within AI-driven travel ecosystems.
Name: Siân Yates, Company: ITIJ, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article reporting on the technology launch

item.title: Insurtech Sytrex raises $4.6 million and rebrands to Braven
item.description:
Braven, formerly Sytrex, an insurtech company, raised $4.6 million in a funding round led by Collide Capital with additional investors including Fiat Ventures, MGV.VC, Carao Ventures, Angeles Ventures, Broom Ventures, and Kuiper Ventures. The company focuses on integrating artificial intelligence technology into insurance operations to enable carriers to control, analyze, and launch innovative data-driven programs. This investment is expected to improve underwriting efficiency and provide carriers with alternative ways to manage risk by deploying AI-enabled solutions that are currently underutilized in the industry.
Name: Carlos Chavez, Company: Braven (formerly Sytrex), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Braven, insurtech company specializing in AI for insurance Name: Navneeta Nandan, Company: The Insurer, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Journalist who authored the article

item.title: Corporate AI investment surges as risk management challenges mount in Hong Kong and China
item.description:
Companies in Hong Kong invested an average of $214 million in AI during the first half of 2026, exceeding the global average, while mainland China firms spent just over $100 million, according to DLA Piper’s survey of 975 business leaders across 16 markets. The study shows Hong Kong companies focus on cautious AI pilot testing within structured frameworks, whereas mainland Chinese firms are advancing rapidly to operational integration, facing distinct risks including vendor dependency, intellectual property, and cybersecurity exposure. This divergence in AI adoption and risk profile affects underwriting and risk management practices by increasing the need for tailored risk assessment tools and capacity adjustments in both markets.
Name: Lauren Hurcombe, Company: DLA Piper, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Technology & Sourcing Group Partner and Global Co-chair at DLA Piper who commented on the survey findings

item.title: Canada wants 60% AI adoption – here's what that means for SMEs and cyber risk
item.description:
BOXX Canada is incorporating AI risk into its cyber insurance pricing despite not currently asking applicants about AI usage, in response to the Canadian government’s plan to increase AI adoption among SMEs from 12% to 60% with over $2 billion in investments. BOXX assumes AI adoption is widespread and factors risks such as data exfiltration, prompt injection, and AI-driven cyber threats into underwriting decisions, emphasizing the need for data governance and mapping before AI deployment. This approach aims to improve underwriting accuracy and risk management, preserving broker margins and carrier capacity as AI reshapes cyber risk exposures.
Name: Neal Jardine, Company: BOXX Canada, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: COO and President of BOXX Canada focusing on cyber insurance and AI risk management Name: Mark Carney, Company: Government of Canada, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Prime Minister of Canada who unveiled the AI adoption investment strategy

item.title: Sixfold introduces AI underwriter to support insurance underwriting decisions
item.description:
Sixfold, an AI solutions provider for insurance, announced the launch of its AI Underwriter platform following a $30 million Series B funding round. The platform uses a core "Underwriting Brain" intelligence that learns from each insurer’s unique underwriting appetite, portfolio data, and historical decisions to generate recommendations, explanations, and next steps during submission processing. This AI integration helps insurers reduce processing times by up to 97%, increase hit ratios by at least 15%, and grow gross written premiums per underwriter by up to 30%, improving underwriting efficiency and capacity.
Name: Taylor Mixides, Company: Reinsurance News, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article covering insurance industry news Name: Alex Schmelkin, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Sixfold responsible for platform vision Name: Melissa Butt, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice President E&S Brokerage, Property, user of the AI Underwriter platform Name: John Burkhart, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President US Property & Casualty, client partner for Sixfold technology Name: Matthew Richardson, Company: Generali GC&C, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Operations & IT, user of Sixfold’s underwriting solutions Name: Jane Tran, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and COO of Sixfold, involved in product development and strategy

item.title: Tugboat Raises $3 Million Seed Round to Expand Homeowner Insurance Claims Platform
item.description:
Tugboat, an insurtech platform supporting homeowners with property insurance claims, raised $3 million in a seed funding round led by ResilienceVC and other investors. The platform uses agentic AI workflows informed by over 10,000 historical claims to guide homeowners through document review, denial disputes, and settlement negotiations for a $99 annual fee. This technology improves claims outcomes, helping users recover substantial additional settlements, thereby increasing loss recovery efficiency and expanding consumer support options amid rising climate-related disaster frequency.
Name: Cameron Mooney, Company: Tugboat, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and CEO of Tugboat focusing on homeowner insurance claims support Name: Aaron Mooney, Company: Tugboat, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder of Tugboat and former insurance adjuster Name: Tahira Dosani, Company: ResilienceVC, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and General Partner at ResilienceVC, lead investor in Tugboat Name: ResilienceVC, Company: ResilienceVC, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Venture capital firm investing in climate resilience and insurtech startups Name: South Dakota First, Company: South Dakota First, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Strategic investor in Tugboat's seed funding round Name: gener8tor, Company: gener8tor, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Startup accelerator and investor participating in Tugboat's seed round Name: Sure Ventures, Company: Sure Ventures, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Investment firm involved in Tugboat's seed funding Name: Acumen America, Company: Acumen America, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Investor focusing on innovative early-stage companies including Tugboat

item.title: New Report Reveals Half of Insurers Have Integrated AI into Core Functions
item.description:
Earnix released the "Insurance 2026: Earnix AI Trends Bulletin | UK Survey Results In Depth," showing that 55% of UK insurers have integrated AI into core functions such as pricing, underwriting, claims, and customer engagement, based on data from over 400 insurance executives globally including 40 UK leaders. The report details widespread adoption of AI with 98% of UK insurers using or planning to use generative AI for unstructured data processing, supported by increased investment in third-party data and a regulatory environment that moderately slows innovation. This integration improves carrier underwriting efficiency and operational workflows but reveals challenges in scaling AI solutions and meeting growing customer personalization expectations, affecting commercial broker margins and competitive capacity.
Name: Adrian Mincher, Company: Earnix, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of UK, Ireland and South Africa at Earnix who commented on AI integration trends in insurance

item.title: "Only an insurance product built with AI can truly insure AI"
item.description:
Lemonade announced that 98% of its insurance policies are sold using artificial intelligence and that over 60% of claims are processed through AI, with a recent product collaboration to insure Tesla’s Full Self-Driving software. The company integrates AI across policy underwriting, claims handling, coding, HR, and finance, using telematics and real-time software updates to dynamically price risk based on behavior and software versions. This AI-driven approach reduces operational costs, improves underwriting accuracy, and introduces new capacity for insuring AI-driven autonomous vehicles, impacting broker margins and carrier efficiency.
Name: Adina Eckstein, Company: Lemonade, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: COO of Lemonade responsible for leveraging AI across insurance operations

item.title: Cybersecurity Forum: Risks Up, Insurance Costs Down
item.description:
Cybersecurity risks for family offices are increasing due to more frequent and sophisticated attacks, with nearly half of US family offices targeted last year, yet cyber insurance premiums are becoming more competitive as firms like Chubb, AIG, and CNA enter the market. The threat landscape has expanded through AI-driven attack methods, social media data mining, and prolonged undetected intrusions, with family offices' informal structures creating vulnerabilities. This dynamic lowers underwriting costs and improves insurance capacity but requires brokers and carriers to adjust risk assessments and coverage offerings for emerging AI-related cyber threats.
Name: Mykolus Rambus, Company: Hush, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of a digital protection firm focusing on cybersecurity. Name: Scott Fogarty, Company: Ridgeback Network Defense, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO providing network defense and cybersecurity services. item.title: Sixfold Launches AI Underwriter -
item.description:
Sixfold has launched its AI Underwriter following a $30 million Series B funding round, designed to assist property and casualty insurers with automated underwriting. The platform uses an AI model called the Underwriting Brain, which integrates carrier-specific data, submission details, broker information, and underwriting guidelines to recommend next steps in underwriting submissions and can be configured for straight-through processing. This technology improves underwriting efficiency, leading to faster processing times, higher hit ratios, and increased gross written premiums per underwriter while maintaining human oversight.
Name: Alex Schmelkin, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of Sixfold Name: Melissa Butt, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Vice President E&S Brokerage, Property at Skyward Specialty Name: John Burkhart, Company: Skyward Specialty, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President US Property & Casualty at Skyward Specialty Name: Matthew Richardson, Company: Generali GC&C, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Operations & IT at Generali GC&C Name: Jane Tran, Company: Sixfold, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Co-founder and COO of Sixfold

item.title: Insurtech Evolves with Strategic Partnerships and Embedded Insurance
item.description:
InsurTech NY held its 2026 Spring Conference in New York City where industry leaders discussed the sector's shift from market disruption toward strategic partnerships and embedded insurance solutions. These developments involve integrating insurance into the customer experience through collaboration between insurers and technology providers, streamlining operations and adopting AI-driven innovations. The evolution increases underwriting efficiency and offers brokers new alternative risk management options, potentially impacting commercial broker margins and expanding carrier capacity.
Name: Ron Johnston, Company: Amwins, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Executive discussing public entity insurance market and parametric insurance as fiscal necessity.
